title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution must not contain duplicate subsets.
hints: For each element, you have two choices: include it or exclude it.
Use backtracking — at each step, decide to take or skip the current element.
Start with empty subset. For each index, add nums[index] to current subset, recurse, then remove it (backtrack). Also recurse without adding.

title: Permutations
description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all possible permutations in any order.
hints: A permutation is an arrangement where order matters and all elements are used. How do you build one?
At each position, choose from the remaining unused elements. Use a visited set or swap technique.
Backtrack: for each position, try each unused number. Add to current permutation, mark used, recurse, then unmark (backtrack).
follow_ups: How would you generate the kth permutation directly?

title: N-Queens
description: Place n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens threaten each other. Return all distinct solutions. Queens attack along rows, columns, and diagonals.
hints: Place queens row by row. For each row, try each column. What constraints must you check?
A queen is safe if no other queen is in the same column, same diagonal, or same anti-diagonal.
Use sets to track occupied columns, diagonals (row-col), and anti-diagonals (row+col). Backtrack when no valid column exists.
follow_ups: Can you count solutions without generating them all?
What optimizations can reduce the search space?

title: Word Search
description: Given an m x n grid of characters and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically). Each cell may be used only once.
hints: Start DFS from every cell that matches the first letter. How do you ensure you don't revisit cells?
Mark cells as visited during the current path. Unmark them when backtracking.
For each cell matching word[0], DFS checking 4 neighbors. Mark cell visited, recurse for next character, unmark on backtrack.
follow_ups: What if you need to find multiple words? (Word Search II with Trie)
How can you prune the search space?
